reviews.review_description:
Really really great environnement, sounds, instruments but PLEASE do an update and add the hand tracking !!!! I m a long time hardware customer since microkorg xl... and afterwards i will promote everywhere your great app ;)
Update : Hand tracking in option in live perf...not needed in production session...Amazing workstation...the more i use it the more i love it. I will choose 5 stars when hand tracking will be an option for the live perf, not for the production.
its impossible tobuse... when im pressing save song, keyboard appeared behind carousel and i can't do nothing just kill app and launch again????? WTF???

Found out by accident I can rotate the carousel with a button combo, with that in mind this app is pretty impressive for what it is. Korg, please upgrade the resolution for later headsets, consider MIDI in over USB (or perhaps through a wireless protocol) and give us an export option. Already a very fun experience and could be actually a useful tool with minor tweaks here and there. Good job Korg

reviews.review_title: Recording function only working on scen1
reviews.review_description: THis has great potential and at €50 thought I was going to get a great product but a real pity there seems to be no community or support to answer questions
This thing is useless basically can only use one control and even then that sucks so there is no way you can do anything live with this like on the iPad ....  I'm getting a refund

I was a bit dubious about this with it still being in development but I'm really glad I gave it a chance. It looks very overwhelming when you see all the dials and buttons but after playing around with it for an hour and looking at the User Manual briefly I managed to make quite a decent start to a track. As with anything new there's a learning curve but I picked it up pretty easily considering I've never used any music production software before. 

I'm hoping that in the future there'll be different loop and sample packs to download for specific genres like Trance, Breakbeat, Hip-Hop etc (although I'm sure with a lot of practice they can be created from scratch anyway) depending on what you want to create. Also I'd love it if there was a way of sampling dialogue from movies to add to the tracks or acapella vocals to add to your tracks would be really cool. But it's still very enjoyable how it is at the moment.

Update 1.1 = Now 16 Gadgets and the sound is good! But it will be more convenient if we can use two hands. I have several synths and find it difficult to use just one hand. with mixed reality it would be perfect.

If you're used to making music in a DAW this is pretty fun.  I can see why people are mad that you can't directly turn the dials but when you're deep in the sound design and focused on the sound the controls work fine.   This app is really fun for sound design and making EDM.  It has most of the synths you would need like a OSC synth and even a cool looking wavetable synth with a position knob.
Tweaking the knobs in VR is pretty fun compared to VSTs.  It feels like you're using the real hardware.   All the familiar controls are here but they are actual knobs which is pretty neat.

You can make some pretty hard sounding beats in this.  The only real problem I have I can only edit the piano roll for one Scene or measure.  There's no way to select the next measure so the only way to edit multiple measures is to copy the and paste and clear the old data.  Which feels like a workaround.   I don't think it's supposed to be like this.
There are some unexpected advanced features like scale highlighting.  This app is fun for producers who make beats in a DAW.  It's a little more hands on than that but you're still placing notes on the piano roll so less hands on than some other VR music apps.

reviews.review_title: A new musical experience ; at discounts
reviews.review_description:
Yes, it is complicated, but this is music composition we are talking about! It would be even more complicated otherwise. (Think staff as musical notation , was it easy?) In perspective, this app is intuitive and amazing!

Even at the price, it is cheaper than a single music lesson, thus a great investment! Still wish a lower price ? Use below tricks to get a 25% discount .

Tips for new users:
For unlimited 25% discounts to 200+ great apps:
google “metadog” for a discount exchange site
or
bio  .   link  /  zzzzz

For advance users: 
google “sidequest” for free apps

Give us a 👍 if you find this helpful

While all of the gadgets you love from the IOS version are there and sound like you’d expect, the user interface is unusable and not intuitive. 

It crashed multiple times before I could open it. Then I entered a room that appears well arranged with tracks surrounding you. But then you try playing notes and they don’t play. You try a bunch of key combinations and finally discover you have to press a two key combination in order to play a note.

So then you try to find a manual or guide, and first you have to figure out the right key combination to open the manual.

Important features that are right out there in the IOS version are hidden in menus unnecessarily, when you have so much more screen real estate in VR.

It seems like whoever is designing this is not a musician, and doesn’t grasp some basic UX/UI standards for VR. Just to rotate oneself requires a two button combination. I’m sorry but asking people to pay $30 for this when it’s not even ready for beta is really upsetting.
This app has great potential, but sorely needs the ability to connect a small midi controller to it so we can play these instruments hands on!

Please add passthrough with mixed reality like Tribe XR! It would be great to be able to see the virtual instruments inside my real room with the cameras. Please include that option in a future update! 

Thanks, I've been waiting for this app for a long time. I hope they give it a lot of update support as they have done on mobile devices.

reviews.review_title: Great VR port of famous Beat Making app!
reviews.review_description:
I recorded my first beat making adventure (before reading the manual, to see how much I could figure out) and here it is!
https://youtu.be/ht_qD6IjbpE
Lots of fun, an app we already love from other devices, in a new octagon shaped room in VR! It's incredible and impressive they were able to do this port.
A good "starting line up" of musical devices are available, enough to make a complete track of your choosing! (I would like the Tokyo drum synth to be added! But the London drum sampler will do for now.)
I've had a great time using it thus far! My one criticism is: Some parts of the User Interface, and the way that our VR hand controllers interact with it are a bit unusual, different from the other VR apps we have been using. For example, I'd want to directly grab knobs and other controls with the trigger and "just move them" as in other VR music apps, but in this case you must first point your laser line at the control to change and click the trigger, then use the right hand thumb stick and buttons to change them. Perhaps more controller modes can be added in future?
Overall, KORG Gadget VR is great! And it should be one of your first VR choices if you like to make electronic or hip hop beats! Looking forward to how the app will progress.

I tried it once before and couldn't get much use. Almost got a sample to play, no interactivity with any samplers / consoles. Tried today and it launches, shows the main environment then crashes to home.

Great Sound and synt modules!

Things that can be worked on: 

Buttons and navigation feels a bit wierd, but it may be more logic after some usage. Also there are some menu options that can`t be selected from the first menu (Options) and only way to get back to that menu is to restart the application?

Graphics is hard to see/read unless you get really close to instruments!
Think it needs some optimization or options to select resolution/supersampling or similar.

The idé of having the synth modules in a VR environment is fine but, UX and workflow needs to be easy to handle. Insted of having everything in a 360 degree layout it should be like a 180 degree layout, with rack and table in front of you! 
Maybe even a 2D MR mode could be added to simplify the work..
Doesn't run with the latest quest OS v57. Shows loading screen and then crashes out. Please fix it devs.

I didn’t find this intuitive @ all..the interface still needs a ton of work. I think it’s great idea and have used the iPad version for years. There just needs to be a lot of improvements made. Please keep working on this though because I’d love to produce in a vr environment.
